old_claim.note: Sternpost Rudders provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No dependency remains because canal locks operate on waterways and gates; they do not require a particular boat steering technology.

source_shape.source_locator: Definition describing gates and a basin that vary water level to raise or lower boats.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies canal locks by the gated water chamber, not by any specific vessel-rudder architecture.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old edge confused vessels using locks with components required to build the lock; sternpost rudders are not part of lock-gate infrastructure.

ontology_before: Canal lock gates depended on a ship-rudder technology as if boat steering were a lock component.
ontology_after: Canal lock gates depend on waterway infrastructure and water-control gate lineage, not vessel steering mechanisms.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: The graph no longer routes canal-lock engineering through ship-rudder technology.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to boats designed specifically for lock navigation.
A source showed the first chamber locks required sternpost-rudder vessels to function.
A future dependent node for lock-compatible boats introduced a correct rudder edge.
short_reason: Sternpost rudders are vessel equipment, not a prerequisite for canal lock gates.
